message:
fix(core): pass bind parameters to count_matching_rows for UPDATE/DELETE

Fix parameterized UPDATE and DELETE statements failing when WHERE clause
contains numbered placeholders (?1, ?2, etc).

## Problem (br-22iss)
count_matching_rows() was called without bind parameters:
  let affected = self.count_matching_rows(&update.table, update.where_clause.as_ref())?;

When the WHERE clause contained placeholders like "WHERE name = ?1",
the preview count query would treat placeholders as NULL, returning 0
affected rows even when matching rows existed.

## Solution
Thread params through to count_matching_rows():
  fn count_matching_rows(
      &self,
      table_ref: &QualifiedTableRef,
      where_clause: Option<&Expr>,
      params: Option<&[SqliteValue]>,  // NEW parameter
  ) -> Result<usize>

Inside count_matching_rows(), use query_with_params() when params exist:
  let rows = if let Some(params) = params {
      self.query_with_params(&sql, params)?
  } else {
      self.query(&sql)?
  };

## Updated call sites
- UPDATE path: count_matching_rows(&update.table, where_clause, params)
- DELETE path: count_matching_rows(&delete.table, where_clause, params)

## Test added: test_update_with_four_numbered_placeholders
Verifies UPDATE with numbered placeholder in WHERE clause:
1. Insert two rows (BlueLake, RedFox)
2. UPDATE with literal WHERE (baseline)
3. UPDATE with "WHERE name = ?1" and bound parameter
4. Assert affected == 1

This matches the exact failure scenario from mcp-agent-mail-db E2E tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
